rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical spirocyclopropanation of indane-1,3-dione with aromatic aldehydes or benzylidenemalononitriles via anodically-generated bromine mediator to afford spiro[indene-cyclopropane]tetraones and dihydrospiro[cyclopropane-indene]dicarbonitriles.
electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces propanol to propoxide (with H2 evolution) generating the base; anode oxidizes Br- to Br2 which acts as a halogenating mediator on the Knoevenagel/Michael adduct enolate.

key_intermediates.name: Michael adduct of two indane-1,3-diones with aldehyde (intermediate 5)

electron_flow: Cathodic reduction of ROH gives RO- (and 1/2 H2) which deprotonates indane-1,3-dione; Knoevenagel condensation with ArCHO followed by Michael addition of a second enolate builds a 1,3-dicarbonyl bis-adduct, whose enolate is brominated by Br2 generated from anodic oxidation of Br-; intramolecular C-alkylation (SN2-like displacement of Br by the second enolate carbon) closes the cyclopropane.

probe_reagents.name: 13C NMR comparison of products 3j vs 9j (C- vs O-cyclization)
probe_reagents.observation: Experimental 13C shifts 37.9 and 51.4 ppm match calculated cyclopropane carbons of 3j (21.6, 51.2); no peak near 108 ppm expected for spirodihydrofuran 9j, confirming intramolecular C-attack (path a) over O-attack (path b).

notes: Authors explicitly considered two divergent cyclization modes from the brominated enolate: path a (C-attack -> cyclopropane 3) vs path b (O-attack -> spirodihydrofuran 9); selectivity for path a supported by calculated vs experimental 13C NMR. An analogous bifurcation is invoked for the malononitrile series (cyclopropane 14 vs chromene-aminonitrile 15). No radical traps, KIE, or CV experiments were reported; mechanism is largely proposed by analogy.
